doom.driver: Anthropic's most dangerous AI model just fell into the wrong hands. Anthropic's Mythos AI model, a powerful cybersecurity tool that the company said could be dangerous in the wrong hands, has been accessed by a "small group of unauthorized users," Bloomberg reports.
doom.summary: The AI model Anthropic literally described as 'dangerous in the wrong hands' has now been accessed by... the wrong hands. Meanwhile, Mythos separately found 271 Firefox security vulnerabilities in what appears to be its free time, and Amazon just handed Anthropic $5 billion to make more of it. The Strait of Hormuz ships-on-fire situation continues its exhausting will-they-won't-they arc, but today's real news is that the cyber-capable AI escaped containment before containment was even finished being built.

factors.name: AI Capability
factors.detail: Anthropic's Mythos — a model capable of finding hundreds of zero-day exploits — was accessed by unauthorized users the same week it publicly humiliated Firefox's security team.

factors.name: Governance
factors.detail: Meta plans to harvest employee keystrokes and mouse movements for AI training, while regulators remain largely absent from the oversight picture.

factors.name: Geopolitics
factors.detail: Iran seized two ships in the Strait of Hormuz hours after Trump extended the ceasefire, and Singapore's foreign minister called Hormuz a 'dry run' for a potential US-China Pacific war.

factors.name: Alignment
factors.detail: An AI model explicitly flagged by its own creators as too dangerous for broad release has now demonstrated that access controls are insufficient — a textbook alignment-adjacent containment failure.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: New gas-powered data centers linked to OpenAI, Meta, and xAI could emit more greenhouse gases annually than entire nations, quietly compounding long-term climate risk.

doom.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, wide aerial view looking down at a 45-degree angle, vast suburban rooftop landscape dominates the frame — a grid of identical early-spring American subdivision rooftops stretches to the horizon under a deep navy sky, bare tree branches just beginning to bud poking between houses, blackout curtains visible in one basement window glowing a hot smoldering orange from within, that single lit room the only warmth in a cold sleeping neighborhood, the aerial perspective making every house look identical except this one, this ordinary ranch-style home radiating something wrong from its screens below — tiny anonymous figure barely visible as a dark hoodie silhouette through the window, hunched over multiple glowing monitors arranged in an arc, the screens casting amber and white terminal-glow light upward through the glass, a corporate lanyard hanging off a monitor corner catching the light like a small flag, a semi-transparent glasswing butterfly sticker barely visible on a laptop lid, elsewhere in the composition a cold fluorescent-blue data center corridor cuts diagonally through the lower third like a slash wound in the earth, rows of black server racks blinking amber and green in geometric precision, deep navy shadows dominate everything, warmth feels threatening, smoke-filtered atmosphere diffuses all light into a heavy orange haze, no text, no antialiasing, 16-bit SNES pixel art.

dawn.driver: Domestic workers legally recognised in Indonesia after '22-year struggle'. The country is home to some 4.2 million domestic workers - of which almost 90% are women.
dawn.blurb: 22 YEARS LATER, 4.2 MILLION WORKERS FINALLY SEE JUSTICE
dawn.summary: Against a backdrop of war, seizures in the Strait of Hormuz, and geopolitical chaos, Indonesia just delivered something quietly extraordinary: legal recognition for 4.2 million domestic workers — mostly women — after a 22-year campaign. This is what stubborn, sustained human solidarity looks like. It won't make the crisis headlines, but for millions of people who cook, clean, and care for others in legal invisibility, today marks a real and irreversible win that no ceasefire can take away.

factors.name: Human Spirit
factors.detail: A 22-year grassroots labor campaign achieving legal recognition for millions of marginalized women workers is a masterclass in sustained, nonviolent social change.

factors.name: Cooperation
factors.detail: Indonesia's parliament passing landmark worker protections signals that democratic institutions can still deliver meaningful justice for the most vulnerable.

factors.name: Science
factors.detail: JWST discovering water-ice clouds on a distant exoplanet and MIT researchers finding a natural protein that may protect the GI tract from infection show discovery marching on regardless of geopolitics.

factors.name: Environment
factors.detail: New AI-driven ocean current mapping (GOFLOW) gives scientists unprecedented visibility into fast-moving sea dynamics, a quiet tool upgrade for climate resilience.

factors.name: Wild Card
factors.detail: A final-stage mRNA bird flu vaccine trial has begun — the same platform technology that defeated COVID is being primed before a potential pandemic even arrives.

dawn.dalle_prompt: Pixel art, 16-bit SNES style, no text, no antialiasing. Weather-dominated scene, warm tropical rain falling in golden amber curtains through equatorial morning light, Jakarta visible as a soft haze of domes and pillars dissolving into mist in the background, the downpour catching sunlight and turning every raindrop into a filament of gold and teal. The atmosphere is primary — thick, luminous, rain-filtered, every element seen through gauze and shimmer. In the middle ground, a group of Indonesian women in simple batik aprons and headscarves stand together in the rain, not sheltering from it, arms around each other, faces tilted upward. The foreground figure holds a crisp legal document pressed to her chest with both worn, calloused hands, eyes closed, a single pixel-bright tear on her cheek catching the amber light. Behind her, a vast soft-focus crowd of women stretches back into the golden rain-haze, faces fading into the weather like a sea dissolving into atmosphere. A broom rests gently against a tiled wall to one side, its handle wet with rain. The number 22 is scratched faintly into the plaster behind it. Deep golden hour amber and warm teal color palette throughout, rain as radiant light, long side-shadows through the downpour, triumphant and tender.
